reforms.description: The Swiss government proposed a draft reform project in 2008 aimed at further deregulating rental regulations, particularly focusing on the indexation of rents to mortgage interest rates, which has been a contentious issue due to its impact on rent volatility.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Deregulation in the housing sector is expected to reduce rental and construction costs, thereby improving overall economic efficiency and productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: A new draft reform project was drawn up by the Confederation in 2008.
reforms.importance_rationale: The proposed reform addresses significant issues in the housing market, aiming to enhance competition and reduce costs, which could have meaningful impacts on the economy.
reforms.source_quote: The government reform of rental regulation must go further.

reforms.description: The Swiss government introduced a co-funding scheme in 2003 to cover up to 30% of the initial investment and operating costs for childcare facilities, aimed at increasing the number of available places for early childhood education.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By increasing the availability of childcare facilities, the scheme supports female labor supply and enhances early childhood education outcomes, which can lead to better long-term economic productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The scheme has been effective in raising the number of available places, which increased by 40% since it was put in place.
reforms.importance_rationale: The co-funding scheme represents a significant effort to improve early childhood education access, which is crucial for long-term educational outcomes and economic productivity.
reforms.source_quote: To address the lack of childcare facilities resulting from deficiencies in funding, the government introduced a co-funding scheme from 2003, covering up to 30% of the initial investment and operating costs for up to 3 years.

reforms.description: The HarmoS concordat aims to lower the start of compulsory free schooling from age 6 to 4, which is expected to enhance early childhood education outcomes, particularly for socially disadvantaged children.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By introducing compulsory schooling at an earlier age, the reform is likely to improve educational outcomes and better prepare children for future academic success, contributing to a more skilled workforce.
reforms.status_evidence: A new cantonal agreement – the HarmoS concordat – will lower the start of compulsory free schooling from 6 to 4 years nation-wide if approved by all cantons.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a structural change in the education system, aiming to enhance early childhood education and address educational disparities, which is crucial for long-term economic growth.
reforms.source_quote: A new cantonal agreement – the HarmoS concordat – will lower the start of compulsory free schooling from 6 to 4 years nation-wide if approved by all cantons.

reforms.description: The Swiss government has prioritized public funding for research, committing to an annual increase of 4.5% in funding until 2015 to enhance education, research, and innovation.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increased public funding for research is expected to enhance innovation capacity and productivity in the long run.
reforms.status_evidence: Funding for education, research, and innovation was granted high priority in the financial plan.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant commitment to enhancing the research landscape in Switzerland, with long-term implications for innovation and economic growth.
reforms.source_quote: In the financial plan 2008-2011 education, research and innovation was granted high priority as funding shall increase by 4.5% per year until 2015.

reforms.description: The Swiss government has proposed reforms to increase private funding for university research, requiring institutions to secure reasonable amounts of third-party financing.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Encouraging private funding for university research is expected to enhance collaboration with industry and improve research outcomes.
reforms.status_evidence: The proposed reform of the higher education landscape (HFKG) requires institutions to acquire reasonable amounts of third party financing.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform is significant as it aims to diversify funding sources for research, which can lead to enhanced innovation and collaboration between universities and the private sector.
reforms.source_quote: The proposed reform of the higher education landscape (HFKG) requires institutions to acquire reasonable amounts of third party financing.
